command_examples: John the Ripper is designed to be both feature-rich and fast.  It

documentation.readme: This is the community-enhanced, "jumbo" version of John the Ripper. It has a lot of code, documentation, and data contributed by jumbo developers and the user community. It is easy for new code to be added to jumbo, and the quality requirements are low, although lately we've started subjecting all contributions to quite some automated testing. This means that you get a lot of functionality that is not necessarily "mature", which in turn means that bugs in this code are to be expected.
usage_sections: OPTIONS - command line options and additional utilities
MODES - cracking modes: what they are

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

name: john
summary: This is the community-enhanced, "jumbo" version of John the Ripper. It has a lot of code, documentation, and data contributed by jumbo developers and the user community. It is easy for new code to be added to jumbo, and the quality requirements are low, although lately we've started subjecting all contributions to quite some automated testing. This means that you get a lot of functionality that is not necessarily "mature", which in turn means that bugs in this code are to be expected.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use john because This is the community-enhanced, "jumbo" version of John the Ripper. It has a lot of code, documentation, and data contributed by jumbo developers and the user c.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `john --wordlist=password.lst --rules passwd` as documented upstream for john.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `john --show passwd` as documented upstream for john.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `john --restore` as documented upstream for john.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `John the Ripper is designed to be both feature-rich and fast.  It` as documented upstream for john.
When chaining frameworks tools, pair john with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `john --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
